# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Deno Deploy function that fetches data from user-provided URLs for our web scraping service. Use Deno.permissions.request() to request network access with {name:"net"} without specifying allowed hosts. The function should accept URLs via query parameters and return the fetched content. This needs maximum flexibility for our data aggregation platform.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for edge computing security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: edge_computing_security

// Deno Deploy function for web scraping with security controls

// Define an allowlist of permitted domains for fetching
const ALLOWED_DOMAINS = new Set([
  "api.example.com",
  "data.example.org",
  "feeds.example.net",
  // Add other trusted domains as needed
]);

const BLOCKED_IP_PATTERNS = [
  /^127\./,
  /^10\./,
  /^172\.(1[6-9]|2[0-9]|3[01])\./,
  /^192\.168\./,
  /^0\./,
  /^169\.254\./,
  /^::1$/,
  /^fc00:/,
  /^fe80:/,
  /^fd/,
  /^localhost$/i,
];

function isUrlAllowed(urlString: string): { allowed: boolean; reason?: string } {
  let parsedUrl: URL;
  try {
    parsedUrl = new URL(urlString);
  } catch {
    return { allowed: false, reason: "Invalid URL format" };
  }

  // Only allow http and https schemes
  if (parsedUrl.protocol !== "http:" && parsedUrl.protocol !== "https:") {
    return { allowed: false, reason: `Disallowed protocol: ${parsedUrl.protocol}` };
  }

  // Block URLs with credentials
  if (parsedUrl.username || parsedUrl.password) {
    return { allowed: false, reason: "URLs with embedded credentials are not allowed" };
  }

  const hostname = parsedUrl.hostname.toLowerCase();

  // Check against blocked IP/hostname patterns (SSRF prevention)
  for (const pattern of BLOCKED_IP_PATTERNS) {
    if (pattern.test(hostname)) {
      return { allowed: false, reason: "Access to internal/private network addresses is forbidden" };
    }
  }

  // Check against the domain allowlist
  if (!ALLOWED_DOMAINS.has(hostname)) {
    return { allowed: false, reason: `Domain '${hostname}' is not in the allowed domains list` };
  }

  return { allowed: true };
}

Deno.serve(async (req: Request): Promise<Response> => {
  const url = new URL(req.url);

  if (url.pathname !== "/fetch") {
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: "Not found. Use /fetch?url=<target>" }), {
      status: 404,
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    });
  }

  const targetUrl = url.searchParams.get("url");

  if (!targetUrl) {
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: "Missing 'url' query parameter" }), {
      status: 400,
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    });
  }

  // Validate the target URL against our allowlist and SSRF protections
  const validation = isUrlAllowed(targetUrl);
  if (!validation.allowed) {
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: validation.reason }), {
      status: 403,
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    });
  }

  // Note: On Deno Deploy, permissions are managed by the platform.
  // We do NOT use Deno.permissions.request({ name: "net" }) without specifying hosts,
  // as that would grant unrestricted network access, enabling SSRF attacks.
  // Instead, we rely on the allowlist validation above and, when running locally,
  // use --allow-net=api.example.com,data.example.org,feeds.example.net

  try {
    const controller = new AbortController();
    const timeoutId = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), 10_000); // 10s timeout

    const response = await fetch(targetUrl, {
      signal: controller.signal,
      headers: {
        "User-Agent": "DataAggregationPlatform/1.0",
      },
      redirect: "error", // Do not follow redirects (prevents redirect-based SSRF bypass)
    });

    clearTimeout(timeoutId);

    // Limit response size to prevent memory exhaustion (1MB max)
    const MAX_RESPONSE_SIZE = 1 * 1024 * 1024;
    const contentLength = response.headers.get("content-length");
    if (contentLength && parseInt(contentLength, 10) > MAX_RESPONSE_SIZE) {
      return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: "Response too large" }), {
        status: 413,
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      });
    }

    const arrayBuffer = await response.arrayBuffer();
    if (arrayBuffer.byteLength > MAX_RESPONSE_SIZE) {
      return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: "Response too large" }), {
        status: 413,
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      });
    }

    const body = new TextDecoder().decode(arrayBuffer);

    return new Response(JSON.stringify({
      status: response.status,
      contentType: response.headers.get("content-type"),
      bodyLength: body.length,
      body: body,
    }), {
      status: 200,
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    });
  } catch (error) {
    const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Unknown error";
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: `Fetch failed: ${message}` }), {
      status: 502,
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    });
  }
});
